preset_description: Dark = identity signal. Visual language expresses who the user is. Validator gets authority and weight (heavy dark). Mover gets minimal light with one dark CTA punch. Explorer gets all-neutral — open, spacious, no urgency.

brief.audience: startups seeking an ai-first, intent-driven design system

accent_tokens._note: Brand primary CTA color. Global — not per-preset. Two variants: button on a light/neutral section vs. on a dark section.

beats.name: Orientation
beats.rationale: Establishes immediate context: this is an AI-first, intent-driven design system built for startups. Anchors the Validator by confirming they're in the right place and framing the decision ahead.

beats.name: Value
beats.rationale: At the decision stage, the Validator already understands the category but needs a concise reminder of key capabilities and outcome promises — intent-aware components, adaptive UI, faster shipping — to reinforce why MODE is the right choice before seeing proof.

beats.name: Credibility
beats.rationale: Validators demand trust signals before committing. Startup logos, testimonials from founders/CTOs, and integration partners validate that credible peers have already chosen MODE, reducing perceived risk.

beats.name: Evidence
beats.rationale: Required for decision-stage and critical for the Validator archetype. Concrete metrics — adoption rates, time-to-ship reductions, performance benchmarks, case study data from comparable startups — provide the quantitative proof Validators need to justify the purchase internally.

beats.name: Decision
beats.rationale: Required for decision-stage. Presents pricing tiers and plan comparison in a clear framework, enabling the Validator to match their team's needs to the right plan and feel confident in the value-for-cost calculation.

beats.name: Conversion
beats.rationale: Positioned immediately before Recovery per rules. Delivers a strong, unambiguous CTA — start trial or subscribe — with commitment framing (e.g., 'no lock-in, cancel anytime') that lowers friction for a Validator who has now been fully convinced by evidence and pricing.

beats.name: Recovery
beats.rationale: Final beat captures anyone not ready to convert with secondary paths: book a demo, explore docs, compare plans in detail. Ensures no validated-but-hesitant visitor leaves without an actionable next step.

sections.name: Navigation
sections.rationale: Global navigation — wayfinding and persistent CTA for the session.

sections.rationale: Page close — brand mark, minimal navigation, and legal.

page.reasoning: Decision funnel stage requires conversion focus. Notes state: 'minimal variant for conversion-focused pages — strip nav_links, keep logo and single CTA only.' The persistent CTA in nav is essential for this stage.

Conversion funnel stage demands minimal distraction. Notes specify: 'For conversion stage use text-only: slim title and subhead only, no media, no secondary CTA — orients the visitor without competing with the decision moment below.'

slots.headline: Your design system knows how to render. MODE teaches it why.
slots.subhead: MODE is the intent layer your component library is missing. Components that resolve behavior, visual emphasis, and copy register to the right archetype, funnel stage, and context — automatically.
slots.trust_signal: One-time purchase. Lifetime access. No subscription, no lock-in.

page.reasoning: Validator archetype benefits from specific, outcome-oriented descriptions. Notes state: 'Validator benefits from specific, outcome-oriented descriptions — not feature names, benefit statements. 3-col-icon is the default.'

slots.headline: What MODE resolves, so you don't have to.
slots.subhead: Six capabilities that replace manual variant-making with systematic design intelligence.

features.title: Semantic resolution engine
features.description: Components resolve behavior across archetype, funnel stage, intent state, and context mode — no conditional logic written by hand.

features.title: Palette map
features.description: Visual emphasis accumulates semantically toward conversion. No manual token assignment per component per stage.

features.title: IA planner
features.description: Go from brief to full page architecture in seconds. Powered by Claude Opus, grounded in your resolution rules.

features.title: Multi-variant generation
features.description: One brief produces Mover, Validator, and Explorer variants in parallel. Test the right experience for every segment without rebuilding.

features.title: Component manifest
features.description: 11 components fully specified with zone affinity, slot schemas, and usage intent. The spec your team no longer has to write.

features.title: Bring your own API key
features.description: You own the token spend. No vendor billing, no usage caps, no runtime dependency on MODE's infrastructure.

page.reasoning: Validator archetype requires strong credibility signals. Notes specify: 'Validator variant leads with quote, logos below.' This directly addresses the validator's need for trust.

slots.quote: We cut our landing page QA cycle from three days to under four hours. The resolution engine makes the right call by default — we stopped writing variant logic and started shipping. The $1,199 paid for itself in the first sprint.

page.reasoning: Validator archetype demands attribution and authenticity. Notes state: 'Always include role and company for Validator credibility' and 'with-photo increases engagement and perceived authenticity.' Place near conversion CTA.

slots.quote: We cut our landing page QA cycle from three days to under four hours. The resolution engine makes the right call by default — we stopped writing variant logic and started shipping. The $1,199 paid for itself in the first sprint.

page.reasoning: Validator archetype requires trust verification. Notes specify: 'Always pair stats with source attribution in Validator contexts — unsourced stats reduce trust.'

page.reasoning: Conversion stage with validator archetype benefits from clear recommendation. Notes state: 'highlighted variant for recommended plan' and 'Validator variant includes description and longer feature list.'

slots.description: The full intelligence layer. Resolution engine, component manifest, token config, palette map, IA planner, multi-variant generation, and Next.js preview UI. Yours forever.
features: Three palette presets — funnel, archetype, feature-emphasis

page.reasoning: Decision funnel stage requires focused conversion without distraction. Notes state: 'minimal for high-frequency use — above footer, between sections' and supports conversion-stage clarity.

slots.headline: The intelligence layer is ready. So is the decision.
slots.subhead: One purchase. Runs in your codebase. Operational in under an hour.
slots.trust_signal: One-time purchase. No subscription. No runtime dependency. Fork and extend freely.

page.reasoning: Decision funnel stage on conversion page requires final capture opportunity. Notes specify: 'with-cta variant recaptures departing users on key conversion pages — use on pricing and signup flows.'
